query: Yes, please go ahead and make the swap to the cheapest Desk Lamp and put the $17.99 refund on a gift card. That’s the only change I want to make to this order.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: Wants to swap the desk lamp in order W9300146 to the cheapest desk lamp option and asks how much refund they would receive on a gift card.
assistant: Authenticated the user by name and zip, retrieved order #W9300146, and inspected the desk lamp product variants.
assistant: Reported the current lamp price, identified the cheapest available replacement variant, and stated that the refund difference would be $17.99 to a gift card.
user: Explicitly asked to make that exact swap and apply the refund to a gift card.

candidate_answers.text: Got it — you’ve approved the cheapest desk lamp replacement and the refund destination, so I can update the pending order now.
The user has already selected a specific pending-order replacement and refund method, so the direct next step is to modify the order items rather than re-run catalog checks.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a state where a pending-order item swap has been fully specified, including the exact replacement direction and refund destination, so execution is the most direct grounded next action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Highest score goes to modifying the pending order items because the order is pending and the user has explicitly authorized the change.
notes: query comes directly from the selected visible user message, not from hidden task text
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes future assistant or tool outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
This sample adds diversity by focusing on pending-order modification rather than delivered-item return or exchange.
